name: Cambil, Huelma, and Arbuniel Loop
name_original: Cambil – vuelta por Huelma y Arbuniel
description: This moderate 27.7-mile road cycling route around Cambil, Huelma, and Arbuniel offers diverse landscapes and cultural stops.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cambil traverse a diverse landscape within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, characterized by majestic mountains and varied ecosystems. The region features extensive olive groves and rolling hills, offering a unique cycling experience through agricultural scenery. River valleys and natural water features add to the scenic beauty, while the topography ranges from gentler paths along riverbeds to strenuous mountain ascents, including the slopes of Monte Almadén. Best road cycling routes around Cambil The most popular road cycling route is Cambil, Huelma, and Arbuniel Loop, a 27.7 miles (44.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a balanced ride through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cambil – La Charca de Pegalajar loop from Cambil, a difficult 70.7 miles (113.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes through mountainous terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Cambil – Cabra del Santo Cristo loop from Cambil, a 61.3 miles (98.6 km) trail leading through the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, often completed in about 5 hours 1 minute. Road cycling around Cambil is defined by mountainous environments, rolling olive groves, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Cambil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from a few reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cambil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Cambil?
A: Road cycling routes around Cambil offer a diverse landscape. You'll encounter majestic mountains and varied ecosystems within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, featuring significant elevation changes. Additionally, many routes traverse extensive olive groves and rolling hills, providing a unique agricultural scenery. The topography ranges from gentler paths along riverbeds to more strenuous mountain ascents, including the slopes of Monte Almadén.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Cambil area?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Cambil, catering to various skill levels. This includes 2 easy routes, 17 moderate routes, and 34 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Cambil?
A: While many routes in Cambil feature significant elevation changes due to the mountainous terrain, there are a few easier options. You can find 2 easy routes among the over 50 available. For a balanced ride through the local countryside, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996234604/cambil-huelma-and-arbuniel-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambil, Huelma, and Arbuniel Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated as moderate and covers 27.7 miles (44.6 km).

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, Cambil offers numerous difficult routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambil – La Charca de Pegalajar loop from Cambil&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 70.7 miles (113.8 km) path with significant elevation changes. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31438594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambil – Cabra del Santo Cristo loop from Cambil&lt;/a&gt;, which is 61.3 miles (98.6 km) and leads through the Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park's stunning landscapes, including vast olive groves and river valleys. You might also encounter natural attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua de Bedmar&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic vistas, some routes offer glimpses of prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, Cambil boasts historical architectural heritage. You can explore the ruins of the Castles of Cambil and Alhabar, perched on rocks overlooking the town. The 16th-century Renaissance-style Church of Nuestra Señora de la Encarnación, along with other historic buildings like the Casa del Hospital and Casa del Condestable, are also within the urban center and accessible from various routes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Cambil?
A: The road cycling routes in Cambil are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountainous environments, the unique experience of riding through rolling olive groves, and the scenic river valleys. The diverse network of routes, offering options from moderate loops to strenuous climbs, is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cambil?
A: Given Cambil's location within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park and its varied terrain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling. These seasons provide milder temperatures, making both the climbs and descents more enjoyable than the heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes available around Cambil?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Cambil are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to explore the region without repeating sections. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996234604/cambil-huelma-and-arbuniel-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambil, Huelma, and Arbuniel Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambil – La Charca de Pegalajar loop from Cambil&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31438594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambil – Cabra del Santo Cristo loop from Cambil&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on road cycling routes in Cambil?
A: Due to Cambil's mountainous environment within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, you can expect significant elevation gains on most road cycling routes. Even moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996234604/cambil-huelma-and-arbuniel-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambil, Huelma, and Arbuniel Loop&lt;/a&gt; feature nearly 850 meters of ascent. More difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambil – La Charca de Pegalajar loop from Cambil&lt;/a&gt;, can involve over 2,100 meters of climbing.
tips:
Cambil is a town that preserves many remains of Arab origin, among which is its castle.
Located in a small valley, it is a cold place in winter but cool in summer.

At the top of the hill that shelters the town stands the castle of Huelma, a 15th-century Christian work carried out after the conquest of the previous Muslim castle in 1438. The town of Huelma is located to the south of Sierra Mágina, in a strategic position between Bélmez, Solera, Cambil and Montejícar, preserving the steps that led to Granada from Jandulilla.
Huelma is a small town that is sheltered by the Loma de las Viñas, a small mountain where the Castillo de los Duques de Alburquerque is located, and where it can be seen from all points of the town.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Cambil – tour around Huelma and Arbuniel route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling tour is rated as moderate. While it is primarily on paved roads, you should be prepared for ascents typical of mountainous regions within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. It's a good challenge for cyclists with some experience.

faqs.question: Is the Cambil – tour around Huelma and Arbuniel route a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Cambil – tour around Huelma and Arbuniel" is designed as a circular route, starting and ending in Cambil, taking you through Huelma and Arbuniel. This allows you to experience the diverse landscapes without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the cycling tour?
faqs.answer: You can typically find public parking in the towns along the route, such as Cambil, Huelma, or Arbuniel. Starting in Cambil, the designated start point, usually offers convenient options for parking your vehicle before heading out on your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and terrain can I expect on this tour?
faqs.answer: The route offers impressive and diverse landscapes within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. You'll cycle through mountainous terrain, passing by forest masses, riparian forests, and mountain cereal fields. Expect to see a variety of trees like poplars, elms, and even rare beech, alongside holm oaks and Aleppo pines in areas like the Dehesa de Los Frailes. The region is also famous for its 'sea of olives'.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to enjoy this road cycling tour are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, allowing you to fully appreciate the natural beauty of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park without the extreme heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Sierra Mágina Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes like this one within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations, stay on designated paths, and be mindful of the natural environment.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The tour connects several interesting locations. You'll pass through the town of Cambil, and in Huelma, you can admire Renaissance monuments and catch a View of Castillo de los Duques de Alburquerque. The route also offers access to the unique flora and fauna of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, with potential glimpses of historical sites like the cortijada of Mata Bejid and the Natural Park Visitor Center.

faqs.question: Is the Cambil – tour around Huelma and Arbuniel route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty and significant elevation gain (around 850 meters), this route is generally more suited for experienced road cyclists rather than families with young children or those looking for a very easy, flat ride. It requires a good level of fitness.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a distinct road cycling tour, sections of this route do intersect or run concurrently with other paths. You might find yourself on parts of the Andalucía - North Route (Província de Jaén), Majada del Rincón, Los tesoros de Arbuniel, or Castillo de Mata Bejid, among others, for short stretches.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through the towns of Cambil, Huelma, and Arbuniel, you'll find opportunities to stop for refreshments. These settlements typically offer local cafes, bars, and restaurants where you can refuel and experience the local cuisine.

faqs.question: Is the Cambil – tour around Huelma and Arbuniel route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling. While dogs are generally allowed in natural parks, bringing a dog on a racebike tour of this distance and difficulty is not typically recommended. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured and that you adhere to all local regulations, especially within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

waypoints.name: View of Castillo de los Duques de Alburquerque, Huelma
